title: From Floer to Hochschild via matrix factorisations
speaker: Jack Smith
abstract: The Hochschild cohomology of the Floer algebra of a Lagrangian L, and the associated closed-open string map, play an important role in the generation criterion for the Fukaya category and in deformation theory approaches to mirror symmetry. I will explain how, in the monotone setting, one can build a map from the Floer cohomology of L with certain local coefficients to (a version of) Hochschild cohomology. This map makes things much more geometric, by transferring the algebraic complexity to the world of matrix factorisations, and is an isomorphism when L is a torus.

proof_stages.label: Toric family-Floer model of the Jacobian ring
proof_stages.summary: The talk begins from the toric fact QH(X) is isomorphic to the Jacobian ring of the toric superpotential W, and recasts this as Floer cohomology of the monotone toric fiber with H_1-valued coefficients together with a computable weighted closed-open map CO_0~.

proof_stages.label: Hochschild generation criterion and the comparison problem
proof_stages.summary: The Fukaya category and quantum cohomology split by the eigenvalues of quantum multiplication by c_1, and the Abouzaid-Sheridan generation criterion reduces split generation to injectivity of the full closed-open map into Hochschild cohomology, motivating the question of relating this hard map to the easier weighted CO_0~ map.

proof_stages.label: General monotone Lagrangian torus setup
proof_stages.summary: Leaving the toric setting, the speaker fixes a monotone Lagrangian torus L, its superpotential W, a critical rank-one local system, the corresponding Fukaya object, its ordinary Floer A-infinity algebra, and the family/Floer complex with H_1 coefficients.

proof_stages.label: Theta comparison, closed-open compatibility, and completion theorem
proof_stages.summary: The main theorems assert an algebra map theta from family Floer cochains with H_1 coefficients to Hochschild cochains of the ordinary Floer algebra, prove that it intertwines CO_0~ with the full closed-open map, and show that after completion at the chosen local system it becomes an isomorphism.

proof_stages.label: Consequences for generation and computation
proof_stages.summary: The comparison identifies family Floer cohomology as a geometric package of completed Hochschild cohomologies, makes the full closed-open map computable from CO_0~, reproves toric generation, and gives new non-toric generation and A-infinity-operation computations.

proof_stages.label: Localized mirror functor and Hochschild zigzag
proof_stages.summary: For the proof, the Cho-Hong-Lau localized mirror functor sends L to a matrix factorization E, inducing a map from Floer cochains to B=End(E) and a zigzag through mixed Hochschild complexes, so theta is obtained from a directly constructed map psi and the completion isomorphism is proved by algebraically computing the matrix-factorization side.

proof_stages.label: Weighted holomorphic-polygon formulas and bubbling argument
proof_stages.summary: The matrix factorization differential is defined by weighting one boundary arc of holomorphic strips so that d^2=W-lambda, the maps phi and psi are defined by analogous weighted holomorphic polygons, and compatibility with closed-open follows by analyzing whether an interior marked point bubbles off on the weighted boundary segment or elsewhere.

examples.name: Constant superpotential and HKR for an exterior algebra
examples.note: When W is constant, the completed family Floer side becomes an exterior algebra tensored with a power series ring, matching Hochschild cohomology by the HKR theorem.
examples.engagement_reason: he traces the constant-potential case through zero differential, completion to exterior algebra tensored with power series, and identifies it via HKR with Hochschild cohomology
